_meta.note: Illustrative offline snapshot for demo and air-gapped use. CVE IDs are real; CVSS is representative and EPSS/KEV flags are point-in-time examples. Run `python run.py update` to refresh against live NVD / CISA KEV / FIRST EPSS.

vulns.title: Global private key extraction
vulns.description: A hardcoded global private key could allow decryption of protected communications and configuration.

vulns.title: Memory protection bypass
vulns.description: A flaw could allow bypass of memory protection to read/write protected regions over the network.

vulns.title: Denial of service via crafted packets
vulns.description: Specially crafted packets to the device could cause it to enter a defect state requiring a manual restart.

vulns.title: Protection relay denial of service
vulns.description: Crafted packets could render a protection relay unresponsive until manually restarted; associated with grid-disruption activity.

vulns.title: Adversary-in-the-middle
vulns.description: An attacker positioned on the network could relay and alter protocol traffic between devices.

vulns.title: Authentication key extraction
vulns.description: A hardcoded key could allow an unauthenticated actor to connect and alter controller configuration or logic.

vulns.title: Boot-code injection
vulns.description: Unsigned boot code could be written to a controller, allowing hidden modification of executed logic.

vulns.title: Authentication bypass (UMAS)
vulns.description: A flaw in the controller management protocol could allow actions without proper authentication.

vulns.title: Resource exhaustion
vulns.description: Crafted requests could exhaust device resources and disrupt availability.

vulns.title: Embedded web server flaw
vulns.description: A weakness in the device web interface could allow unauthorised actions.

vulns.title: Log4Shell remote code execution
vulns.description: A logging library flaw allowing remote code execution; present in many SCADA/historian and Java-based OT applications.

vulns.title: TCP/IP stack remote code execution
vulns.description: A flaw in the embedded TCP/IP stack used by many field devices could allow remote code execution.

vulns.title: Licensing component memory corruption
vulns.description: A widely-embedded licensing component flaw could allow remote code execution; leveraged by cross-vendor ICS tooling.

vulns.title: EtherNet/IP stack overflow
vulns.description: A stack overflow in a widely-licensed EtherNet/IP implementation could allow remote code execution or denial of service.

vulns.title: Dirty Pipe privilege escalation
vulns.description: A kernel flaw allowing local privilege escalation on Linux-based HMIs, gateways, and engineering hosts.

vulns.title: PrintNightmare remote code execution
vulns.description: A Windows print spooler flaw allowing remote code execution on engineering workstations and operator stations.

vulns.title: EternalBlue SMBv1 remote code execution
vulns.description: An SMBv1 flaw enabling worming remote code execution; historically devastating on flat OT networks.

vulns.title: Web interface injection
vulns.description: Insufficient input handling in the device web interface could allow injection of unintended content.
